clinical_notes.summary: 52yo male with Type 2 DM (12 years), HTN, obesity (BMI 34.8), and peripheral neuropathy presenting with 2-month worsening fatigue, 3-week intermittent blurred vision, and progressive bilateral foot numbness. Home glucose frequently >250 mg/dL despite medications. Poor dietary adherence and inconsistent exercise. Non-healing 1.5cm ulcer on plantar right great toe x2 weeks. S4 gallop on exam. Decreased monofilament sensation below ankles bilaterally. BP 152/94 mmHg. No drug allergies. Family history of DM2 and MI (father at 60).

chest_xray.summary: Cardiomegaly with cardiothoracic ratio 0.56, consistent with hypertensive heart disease. Lung fields clear bilaterally, no infiltrates or effusions. Mildly prominent aortic knob. No acute pulmonary abnormality. Body habitus limits radiographic detail.

ecg.summary: Normal sinus rhythm at 82 bpm. Left axis deviation (-30 degrees). Left ventricular hypertrophy by voltage criteria (Sokolow-Lyon index >35mm). ST-segment depression and T-wave inversions in lateral leads (V5-V6, I, aVL) consistent with LVH with strain pattern. PR interval 172ms. QRS duration 96ms. QTc 428ms. No acute ischemic changes.

echo.summary: Concentric left ventricular hypertrophy with increased wall thickness (IVSd 13mm, PWd 12mm). LVEF 50% (low-normal, mildly reduced from hypertensive cardiomyopathy). Diastolic dysfunction grade II (pseudonormal pattern) — E/e' ratio 14, suggesting mildly elevated filling pressures. No significant valvular disease. Trace mitral regurgitation. Normal right heart. No pericardial effusion.

heart_sounds.summary: Regular rate and rhythm at 82 bpm. S4 gallop present at apex, consistent with reduced LV compliance from LVH. S1 and S2 normal. No murmurs or rubs. Mild pedal edema noted. S4 reflects stiff, hypertrophied ventricle consistent with hypertensive cardiomyopathy.

lab_results.summary: HbA1c: 10.2% (markedly elevated, poorly controlled DM2). Fasting glucose: 268 mg/dL. BMP: creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, eGFR 58 mL/min (CKD stage 3a). Potassium 4.6 mEq/L. Urine albumin/creatinine ratio: 280 mg/g (moderately elevated, diabetic nephropathy). LDL 118 mg/dL. Triglycerides 242 mg/dL (elevated). Hemoglobin 13.1 g/dL (mild anemia of chronic disease). CBC otherwise normal. Wound culture from toe ulcer pending.

medication.summary: Active medications: Metformin 1000mg BID (DM2), Glipizide 10mg BID (DM2), Lisinopril 20mg daily (HTN + diabetic nephropathy), Amlodipine 10mg daily (HTN), Atorvastatin 40mg daily (hyperlipidemia). No allergies. Recommendations: add SGLT2 inhibitor (empagliflozin) for cardiorenal benefit; consider GLP-1 agonist for weight loss and glycemic control; increase atorvastatin to 80mg; intensify DM management with endocrinology referral. Podiatry referral for toe ulcer.
